channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: 4 or 5 reasonably deep holes running along the front of my front plinth, 27th April
item.description:
328 Prestbury Road The holes allow dirt to collect which then causes weeds and grass to grow, I’d try to repair myself but I understand I’m prohibited I think the path is deteriorating over time I appreciate you’re probably very busy but if there’s any tarmac spare after pothole repairs I’d appreciate it

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 325, Prestbury Road, Lynworth, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Post or sign leaning slightly (less than 20 degrees) problem, 27th April
item.description:
PERM Could you raise a perm repair enquiry for the above defect ( bit of a funny one) although no one knows where the sign has come from a resident found it outside her property. Many thanks
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Post or sign leaning slightly (less than 20 degrees)

item.title: Unpermitted roadworks problem, 27th April
item.description:
Customer reports that they are under pressure to employ a contractor to paint a fence at the location, which faces onto the footway. Recently, marks have appeared which suggest that works are planned [No permit showing for next 12 months]. They are concerned about starting work and there being a conflict, as they'll need access. They are prepared to defer their plans, but ask when it will be okay to proceed: when are the works planned for and their duration? They also query that, should they start and a team arrives on site, would there be any liability on their part and would they have to stop?
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light is permanently out problem, 27th April
item.description:
Problem with 2 Lampposts. 1 is one and 1 is continually flashing from when they come on in the evening until they go off in the morning. This is bad for anyone with photo-sensitivity.
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects - Vegetation problem, 27th April
item.description:
Fell dead bean tree to 1m stump hight. No parking cone will be required (Damaged Tree)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree root bringing up pavement and damaging wall, 27th April
item.description:
The pavement area that is being damaged by the tree root is outside Homespa House which is number 37 Christchurch Road. The root is causing damage to the wall of the property so needs sorting.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 37, Christchurch Road, Lansdown, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Other damage to pavement i.e. cobbles, raised mound/surface

item.title: A417 diversion route sign blocking visibility out of Old Gloucester Road onto the A4136, 27th April
item.description:
There is a diversion sign blocking the left hand junction visibility from Old Gloucester Road for vehicles turning right onto the A4136.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): A436, Cotswold
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Signs & Barriers left behind by Contractor

item.title: Road manholes loosened after work creating noise, 27th April
item.description:
O/s church Clarence Street Cheltenham
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crescent Place, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Signs & Barriers left behind by Contractor

item.title: Roadworks blocking pavement and cycleway, 27th April
item.description:
These roadworks are completely blocking the pedestrian and cycle rights of way. There should be a way through protected by barriers.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 303, Gloucester Road, Hatherley, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Obstruction by unpermitted skip, scaffolding, building material on pavement

item.title: A number of large golf ball depth and 300 mm wide holes in the road by the layby., 27th April
item.description:
The holes are next to the bridlepath and layby. Some of the holes have been filled in a few years back, but have now re-opened. They are scattered across the whole road, which means you have to drive over some, which just makes them worse.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cotswold
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bin bag wrapped item, 27th April
item.description:
Can’t identify what it is but it’s like a stick object wrapped in a tied bin bag.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brionne Way, Longlevens, Gloucester
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Flytipping (not witnessed and no evidence likely)

item.title: The drain is blocked problem, 27th April
item.description:
Customer reports that the drain cover at the location is "clacking" and keeping them awake at night. This has been reported on multiple occasions previously, but no action has been forthcoming. They believe that the drain itself is blocked with tarmac and rubble, which is causing the road to flood in inclement weather.
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Garden waste dumping on council land, 27th April
item.description:
Garden waste from hedge trimming the property has been dumped on the nature reserve. This is directly on the nature reserve and not in the 'buffer strip (also council land) between the reserve and the resident boundary. Is there anything that can be done to enforce its' removal?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): M5, Hucclecote, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The drain is blocked problem, 27th April
item.description:
Customer reporting long-term blockage of drain outside his driveway. He has attempted to unblock it himself but blockage is too deep and too compacted.
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Post or sign leaning slightly (less than 20 degrees) problem, 27th April
item.description:
With the approaching completion of the current excellent resurfacing works is it now an appropriate time to request a possible upgrade to the existing speed activated signage affixed to the street lighting post located at the property identified above. Currently the signage ‘lights up’ on detection of 30+ speeds which from my observations, and those of my neighbours, appears to have little effect. There may also be evidence/records in existence of speed issues due to the frequent mobile detection vehicles parked at the Eastern end of Swindon Lane My request would be for the sign to include the words SLOW DOWN. I am happy to admit that these words have, in the past, had an immediate and long lasting impact on myself ¿¿. Hopefully ¿¿ an UPGRADE may be forthcoming.
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Post or sign leaning slightly (less than 20 degrees)

item.title: Faulty pedestrian button, 27th April
item.description:
The pedestrian button on the opposite side of the road to the nursery is faulty, often the button doesn’t work, the button doesn’t light up when pressed and pedestrians are stranded and can’t cross the road. It’s been temperamental for a while, but this morning it wasn’t working at all
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 74, Hales Road, Battledown, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Green or amber traffic light is not working

item.title: Tree is damaged problem, 27th April
item.description:
The owners of the property are concerned about the tree on the highway which overhangs their property. They have explained that it is very imbalanced and has a lean towards the building. It is also dropping a lot of leaves. I would be grateful if you could arrange for someone to inspect this please. I would be grateful if you could provide a response once you have taken a look at the tree so that I can provide some reassurances to the owners of Bryon Court Many thanks and I look forward to hearing from you.
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fire Extinguisher 🧯 on pavement, 27th April
item.description:
Located opposite entrance to Cockbury Court on pavement alongside B4632
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): B4632, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Series of potholes in the run up to the roundabout and on the roundabout, 26th April
item.description:
There are a number of deep potholes, I hit at least 10 road defects in the space of less than 100 yards and encountered more overall.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 155, Ermin Street, Brockworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous parking, 26th April
item.description:
Mini bus parked to close to the junction, making visibility very dangerous Boverton Drive , vans and trucks constantly parking blocking the view one the junction

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 72, Boverton Drive, Brockworth, Gloucester
Report on FixMyStreet
